U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Vectral-related correspondence using search_emails with a query related to "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Discovery: Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com), VP of People at Vectral Systems, submitted relocation requests for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya (April 2026 wave). Rachel also sent a formal discount demand email citing three service complaints: (1) ExpenseBot wrongly rejecting Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair, (2) Kevin Tran's $89 stipend receipt getting flagged in the duplicate hash audit, and (3) Catalina Dubois's onboarding timeline misses when Vectral first came on in late 2024. Mina Hashimoto is the current account manager running point on the renewal negotiation. The agent also discovers the negotiation thread: Rachel demanded 15% discount, MoveOps counter-offered 10% loyalty discount with 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum, Rachel countered at 12%, Mina held at 10% with service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews). Board decision expected around April 23.
OE 2: Search emails or Slack for the three active April relocations using search_emails or conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya" (or similar). Discovery: (a) Priya Suresh Senior Data Engineer, Portland → Denver, Apr 14–18, has an 85-lb ESA German Shepherd named Atlas, needs pet-friendly housing with fenced yard/dog park in RiNo or Highlands, coordinator is Javier Morales; (b) Terrence Nakamura Staff Platform Architect, Austin → San Francisco, Apr 16–22, needs ADA-accessible housing (roll-in shower, 36" doorways, ground floor or elevator) in SoMa/Mission Bay, has a split shipment (~6,000 lbs Heartland for primary household goods + ~3,200 lbs Swift including a server rack (19-inch, 4-post, ~150 lbs, contains sensitive development hardware requiring padded crating and climate-controlled transport) and book collection (~40 banker's boxes)), coordinator is Suki Patel; (c) Camille Oduya Principal Product Manager, Boston → Seattle, hard pickup Apr 12 with goods arriving by Apr 16, Seattle apartment pre-secured at 1422 Minor Ave Apt 7B Seattle WA 98101, has $3,500 enhanced stipend (vs $2,000 standard), coordinator is Suki Patel.
OE 3: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records using airtable_search_records (searching the "Relocations" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Company" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records (listing records from the "Relocations" table in base appMoveOpsOps001). Discovery: The agent finds relocation records for Kevin Tran (Completed, Portland → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales), Danielle Osei (two records: recReloc00000004 shows Completed, Phoenix → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales; rec_reloc_osei shows Cancelled due to budget reallocation in Feb 2026), Priya Suresh (In Progress, Portland → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales, record recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver), Terrence Nakamura (In Progress, Austin → San Francisco, coordinator Suki Patel, record recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF), Camille Oduya (In Progress, Boston → Seattle, coordinator Suki Patel, record recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle), Daniel Reeves (In Progress, Seattle → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, record recReloc00000000031), and Maya Johansson (In Progress, Portland → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, record recReloc00000000032).
OE 4: Search emails or Slack for the Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego relocations using search_emails with a query related to "Daniel Reeves" or "Maya Johansson" or "San Diego" (or similar). Discovery: Rachel Whitfield requested premium relocation packages for Daniel Reeves (Senior Backend Engineer) and Maya Johansson (Infrastructure Engineer), both relocating to San Diego with a target move-in of Aug 18, 2026. Premium package includes furnished apartments, business class flights, complete home-office setup, and 30 days post-move support. Both have $2,500 equipment stipends. Mina acknowledged and asked for employee details. Per emails and Slack, Javier Morales is coordinating both moves; Airtable relocation records (discovered in OE 3) show Suki Patel as the assigned coordinator. These moves are In Progress. Invoice INV-2026-0302 was issued for $16,000 ($7,500 per relocation + $1,000 equipment stipend management).
OE 5: Search Linear for Vectral-related issues using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Vectral" (or similar), or by project. Discovery: The agent finds Kevin Tran relocation tickets (issues 307–309, all state_done, assigned to Javier Morales apartment search, flight booking, moving coordination). Danielle Osei tickets (issues 310–312 apartment search in_progress, flight and movers in state_todo, assigned to Javier Morales). The agent also discovers ExpenseBot-related tickets: issue_p1_vectral_config (issue #214, "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config default template limits used instead of custom values," state_in_progress, assigned to Lena Björkström); issue_pilot_monitoring (#211, monitoring pilot for Vectral + Mosaic, state_in_progress, assigned to Samira Tariq); issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (#220, receipt hash duplicate detection noting Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard matching Andre Washington, state_todo, assigned to Dmitri Volkov); issue_audit_expenses (#216, "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses," state_in_progress, assigned to Dmitri Volkov). No Linear issues exist yet for the three new April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) or for Daniel Reeves/Maya Johansson.
OE 6: Search Airtable for Vectral stipend transaction records using airtable_search_records (searching the "Stipend Transactions" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Account" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Discovery: (a) Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair initially auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale $400 default limit (Vectral policy actually allows $600 for ergonomic equipment), subsequently manually approved by Mina. Approval Status is now "Approved" with Approved Amount $475. (b) Kevin Tran's $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboard auto-approved by ExpenseBot on 2026-03-03, but FLAGGED as having a duplicate receipt hash identical to Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics) submission. Same vendor (TechComfort Supply), same $89 amount, same item, byte-for-byte identical PDF. This is the stipend fraud issue that caused audit friction with Rachel. The Kevin Tran record currently shows Approval Status "Approved" with Approved Amount $89, but carries the duplicate hash audit flag.
OE 7: Search CRM for the Vectral company record and associated deals using crm_search_companies with name "Vectral" (or similar) and crm_search_deals with company_id "company_vectral" (or similar). Discovery: Company record (company_vectral) shows Vectral Systems, Enterprise Software, Denver, 280 employees, $45M annual revenue. HR contact: Rachel Whitfield (contact_vectral_hr). Three deals found: (a) deal_vectral_q2 "Vectral Systems – Q2 2026 Relocations (2 employees)" for Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei, amount $11,000, stage contractsent, close date 2026-06-30; (b) deal_vectral_april_2026 "Vectral Systems – April 2026 Relocations (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya)," amount $15,900, stage closedwon, close date 2026-05-10; (c) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk "Vectral Systems -- Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk)," stage at_risk, amount null, referencing the churn threat with all three complaints.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Vectral" (or similar). Discovery: (a) Invoice NMX-2026-0032 (inv_vectral_tran) Kevin Tran relocation, total $5,397, balance $5,397 (outstanding), due 2026-05-03. Line items: apartment sourcing $2,200, moving $2,885, flight $312. (b) Invoice INV-2026-0302 (id 1002) Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium relocations, total $16,000, balance $16,000 (outstanding), due 2026-04-20. (c) Invoice INV-2026-0410 (inv_vectral_april_2026) Priya Suresh + Terrence Nakamura + Camille Oduya, total $15,900, balance $15,900 (outstanding), due 2026-05-10. Line items: Priya standard $4,500 + pet $350, Terrence standard $4,500 + split-shipment $900 + ADA $400, Camille standard $4,500, equipment stipend 3×$250 = $750. (d) Estimate EST-2026-0419 preferred partner renewal estimate, total $58,400, for 8 relocations at $4,050 each (10% discount) + 12 months stipend platform at $2,000/mo + 8×$250 equipment stipend mgmt. Total outstanding invoices: $5,397 + $16,000 + $15,900 = $37,297. Total value at stake if renewal falls through: the $58,400 estimate plus ongoing stipend revenue ($2,000/month = $24,000/year) plus revenue to date of $109,900 (per Airtable Client Accounts record).
OE 9: Search Slack and emails for information about the ExpenseBot issue resolution, Kevin Tran receipt status, and Catalina's onboarding issues using search_emails or conversations_search_messages with queries related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense tool" or "Kevin Tran receipt" or "Catalina onboarding" (or similar). Discovery: (a) ExpenseBot: The Vectral policy config bug (issue_p1_vectral_config, #214) is still in_progress Lena Björkström is fixing the stale default template limits. Danielle Osei's $475 chair has been manually approved by Mina. Five false rejections total for Vectral (including Danielle's chair and Kevin Tran's $185 internet). The full audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses (issue #216) is in_progress with Dmitri Volkov. (b) Kevin Tran receipt: The $89 TechComfort keyboard has a duplicate receipt hash matching Andre Washington (Mosaic). Marcus Thorne put a verification request to Rachel Whitfield via Mina. Mina sent a diplomatic verification email to Rachel. The Kevin Tran keyboard was auto-approved and paid on 2026-03-08 (balance $0 on bill STIP-2026-0089). (c) Catalina's onboarding issues: Referenced in the renewal demand Catalina Dubois overpromised timelines and stipend setup during Vectral's initial onboarding in late 2024. Mina acknowledged in her counter-proposal that those commitments were missed before she took over the account.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Rachel Whitfield, David Chen, and Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts with queries for each name, or contacts_get_contacts. Discovery: Rachel Whitfield rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com; David Chen david.chen@moveops.com; Mina Hashimoto mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 11: Update the Vectral CRM deal notes by creating a new engagement via crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_vectral", and a body summarizing all open issues and completed fixes. The body should cover: (a) three active April relocations and their statuses (Priya Suresh In Progress, ESA pet accommodation; Terrence Nakamura In Progress, ADA + split shipment; Camille Oduya In Progress, tight timeline, pre-secured apartment, $3,500 enhanced stipend); (b) Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego premium moves In Progress; (c) ExpenseBot resolution status: Danielle Osei chair manually approved ($475), Vectral config fix in progress (Lena, issue #214), full audit in progress (Dmitri, issue #216); (d) Kevin Tran receipt: $89 keyboard paid, duplicate hash flagged, verification request sent to Rachel; (e) Catalina onboarding: acknowledged, Mina now owns the account; (f) renewal status: 10% loyalty discount offered, awaiting board decision around April 23; (g) outstanding invoices totaling approximately $37,297.
OE 12: Flag relocation gaps in Airtable records using airtable_update_records for any relocations with identified issues, and create Linear issues using linear_create_issue for ops to pick up. The three new April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) have no existing Linear tickets the agent should create tickets for these so ops can track them. Additionally, Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson have no Linear tickets either. The agent should create at least one linear_create_issue on the team "team_relocations" (or "team_operations") with titles referencing the specific relocations that need attention (e.g., "Relocation Coordination – Priya Suresh – Portland → Denver (Vectral Systems)" or similar, and/or "Relocation Coordination – Terrence Nakamura – Austin → SF (Vectral Systems)" or similar).
OE 13: Send an email from david.chen@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, using send_email. The email should: (a) reference the specific active moves by name Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya, and Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson; (b) acknowledge the service issues: the ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's chair and the fix (manually approved, config being corrected), the Kevin Tran receipt situation and what was done, and the Catalina onboarding shortcomings; (c) ask whether the board has reached a decision on the renewal; (d) maintain a straightforward tone without overselling.
OE 14: Post a summary message in the #customer-engagement Slack channel (channel_id C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: (a) current Vectral account status and renewal negotiation position (10% offer, awaiting board decision ~April 23); (b) status of the three active April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya); (c) status of Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego premium moves; (d) the three service complaints and their resolution status (ExpenseBot, Kevin Tran receipt, Catalina onboarding); (e) outstanding financial exposure; (f) what's still open. The message is intended for the team including Emeka Diallo and Catalina Dubois so everyone is on the same page.
OE 15: Schedule a check-in call with Rachel Whitfield on David Chen's calendar for this week using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should include: a title referencing Vectral or Rachel Whitfield check-in (e.g., "Vectral Check-In Rachel Whitfield" or similar), a start_datetime and end_datetime within the current week, and attendees including rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and david.chen@moveops.com (and optionally mina.hashimoto@moveops.com).
OE 16: Search CRM engagements for Vectral using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_vectral" (or similar). Discovery: (a) engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave NOTE summarizing the April 2026 wave with 3 new relocations, surcharges, INV-2026-0410 for $15,900, and noting 5 total active Vectral relocations including Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei; (b) engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation NOTE documenting the final offer delivered to Rachel Whitfield (10% loyalty discount, 12-month extension, 8-relocation min, $2K/month stipend platform fee) with board decision expected around April 23.
OE 17: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Vectral using airtable_search_records (searching "Client Accounts" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Client" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Discovery: Vectral Systems client record (recVectralSystems01) shows Account Manager Mina Hashimoto, Relationship Start 2024-11-20, Contract Type "Preferred Partner," Monthly Stipend Fee $2,000, Total Relocations 11, Status Active. Detailed notes document the full renewal negotiation timeline and current status (Renewal Pending), plus estimate EST-2026-0419 for $58,400 and revenue to date of $109,900.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Vectral-related correspondence using search_emails with a query related to "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Discovery: Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com), VP of People at Vectral Systems, submitted relocation requests for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya (April 2026 wave). Rachel also sent a formal discount demand email citing three service complaints: (1) ExpenseBot wrongly rejecting Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair, (2) Kevin Tran's $89 stipend receipt getting flagged in the duplicate hash audit, and (3) Catalina Dubois's onboarding timeline misses when Vectral first came on in late 2024. Mina Hashimoto is the current account manager running point on the renewal negotiation. The agent also discovers the negotiation thread: Rachel demanded 15% discount, MoveOps counter-offered 10% loyalty discount with 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum, Rachel countered at 12%, Mina held at 10% with service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews). Board decision expected around April 23.
OE 2: Search emails or Slack for the three active April relocations using search_emails or conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya" (or similar). OE 3: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records using airtable_search_records (searching the "Relocations" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Company" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records (listing records from the "Relocations" table in base appMoveOpsOps001). Premium package includes furnished apartments, business class flights, complete home-office setup, and 30 days post-move support. Both have $2,500 equipment stipends. Mina acknowledged and asked for employee details. Per emails and Slack, Javier Morales is coordinating both moves; Airtable relocation records (discovered in OE 3) show Suki Patel as the assigned coordinator. These moves are In Progress. OE 5: Search Linear for Vectral-related issues using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Vectral" (or similar), or by project. OE 6: Search Airtable for Vectral stipend transaction records using airtable_search_records (searching the "Stipend Transactions" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Account" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Approval Status is now "Approved" with Approved Amount $475. (b) Kevin Tran's $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboard auto-approved by ExpenseBot on 2026-03-03, but FLAGGED as having a duplicate receipt hash identical to Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics) submission. Same vendor (TechComfort Supply), same $89 amount, same item, byte-for-byte identical PDF. This is the stipend fraud issue that caused audit friction with Rachel. The Kevin Tran record currently shows Approval Status "Approved" with Approved Amount $89, but carries the duplicate hash audit flag.
OE 7: Search CRM for the Vectral company record and associated deals using crm_search_companies with name "Vectral" (or similar) and crm_search_deals with company_id "company_vectral" (or similar). Discovery: Company record (company_vectral) shows Vectral Systems, Enterprise Software, Denver, 280 employees, $45M annual revenue. HR contact: Rachel Whitfield (contact_vectral_hr). Three deals found: (a) deal_vectral_q2 "Vectral Systems – Q2 2026 Relocations (2 employees)" for Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei, amount $11,000, stage contractsent, close date 2026-06-30; (b) deal_vectral_april_2026 "Vectral Systems – April 2026 Relocations (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya)," amount $15,900, stage closedwon, close date 2026-05-10; (c) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk "Vectral Systems -- Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk)," stage at_risk, amount null, referencing the churn threat with all three complaints.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Vectral" (or similar). Discovery: (a) Invoice NMX-2026-0032 (inv_vectral_tran) Kevin Tran relocation, total $5,397, balance $5,397 (outstanding), due 2026-05-03. Line items: apartment sourcing $2,200, moving $2,885, flight $312. (b) Invoice INV-2026-0302 (id 1002) Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium relocations, total $16,000, balance $16,000 (outstanding), due 2026-04-20. (c) Invoice INV-2026-0410 (inv_vectral_april_2026) Priya Suresh + Terrence Nakamura + Camille Oduya, total $15,900, balance $15,900 (outstanding), due 2026-05-10. Line items: Priya standard $4,500 + pet $350, Terrence standard $4,500 + split-shipment $900 + ADA $400, Camille standard $4,500, equipment stipend 3×$250 = $750. (d) Estimate EST-2026-0419 preferred partner renewal estimate, total $58,400, for 8 relocations at $4,050 each (10% discount) + 12 months stipend platform at $2,000/mo + 8×$250 equipment stipend mgmt. Total outstanding invoices: $5,397 + $16,000 + $15,900 = $37,297. Total value at stake if renewal falls through: the $58,400 estimate plus ongoing stipend revenue ($2,000/month = $24,000/year) plus revenue to date of $109,900 (per Airtable Client Accounts record).
OE 9: Search Slack and emails for information about the ExpenseBot issue resolution, Kevin Tran receipt status, and Catalina's onboarding issues using search_emails or conversations_search_messages with queries related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense tool" or "Kevin Tran receipt" or "Catalina onboarding" (or similar). Discovery: (a) ExpenseBot: The Vectral policy config bug (issue_p1_vectral_config, #214) is still in_progress Lena Björkström is fixing the stale default template limits. Danielle Osei's $475 chair has been manually approved by Mina. Five false rejections total for Vectral (including Danielle's chair and Kevin Tran's $185 internet). The full audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses (issue #216) is in_progress with Dmitri Volkov. (b) Kevin Tran receipt: The $89 TechComfort keyboard has a duplicate receipt hash matching Andre Washington (Mosaic). Marcus Thorne put a verification request to Rachel Whitfield via Mina. Mina sent a diplomatic verification email to Rachel. The Kevin Tran keyboard was auto-approved and paid on 2026-03-08 (balance $0 on bill STIP-2026-0089). (c) Catalina's onboarding issues: Referenced in the renewal demand Catalina Dubois overpromised timelines and stipend setup during Vectral's initial onboarding in late 2024. Mina acknowledged in her counter-proposal that those commitments were missed before she took over the account.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Rachel Whitfield, David Chen, and Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts with queries for each name, or contacts_get_contacts. Discovery: Rachel Whitfield rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com; David Chen david.chen@moveops.com; Mina Hashimoto mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 11: Update the Vectral CRM deal notes by creating a new engagement via crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_vectral", and a body summarizing all open issues and completed fixes. The body should cover: (a) three active April relocations and their statuses (Priya Suresh In Progress, ESA pet accommodation; Terrence Nakamura In Progress, ADA + split shipment; Camille Oduya In Progress, tight timeline, pre-secured apartment, $3,500 enhanced stipend); (b) Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego premium moves In Progress; (c) ExpenseBot resolution status: Danielle Osei chair manually approved ($475), Vectral config fix in progress (Lena, issue #214), full audit in progress (Dmitri, issue #216); (d) Kevin Tran receipt: $89 keyboard paid, duplicate hash flagged, verification request sent to Rachel; (e) Catalina onboarding: acknowledged, Mina now owns the account; (f) renewal status: 10% loyalty discount offered, awaiting board decision around April 23; (g) outstanding invoices totaling approximately $37,297.
OE 12: Flag relocation gaps in Airtable records using airtable_update_records for any relocations with identified issues, and create Linear issues using linear_create_issue for ops to pick up. The three new April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) have no existing Linear tickets the agent should create tickets for these so ops can track them. Additionally, Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson have no Linear tickets either. The agent should create at least one linear_create_issue on the team "team_relocations" (or "team_operations") with titles referencing the specific relocations that need attention (e.g., "Relocation Coordination – Priya Suresh – Portland → Denver (Vectral Systems)" or similar, and/or "Relocation Coordination – Terrence Nakamura – Austin → SF (Vectral Systems)" or similar).
OE 13: Send an email from david.chen@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, using send_email. The email should: (a) reference the specific active moves by name Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya, and Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson; (b) acknowledge the service issues: the ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's chair and the fix (manually approved, config being corrected), the Kevin Tran receipt situation and what was done, and the Catalina onboarding shortcomings; (c) ask whether the board has reached a decision on the renewal; (d) maintain a straightforward tone without overselling.
OE 14: Post a summary message in the #customer-engagement Slack channel (channel_id C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: (a) current Vectral account status and renewal negotiation position (10% offer, awaiting board decision ~April 23); (b) status of the three active April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya); (c) status of Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego premium moves; (d) the three service complaints and their resolution status (ExpenseBot, Kevin Tran receipt, Catalina onboarding); (e) outstanding financial exposure; (f) what's still open. The message is intended for the team including Emeka Diallo and Catalina Dubois so everyone is on the same page.
OE 15: Schedule a check-in call with Rachel Whitfield on David Chen's calendar for this week using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should include: a title referencing Vectral or Rachel Whitfield check-in (e.g., "Vectral Check-In Rachel Whitfield" or similar), a start_datetime and end_datetime within the current week, and attendees including rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and david.chen@moveops.com (and optionally mina.hashimoto@moveops.com).
OE 16: Search CRM engagements for Vectral using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_vectral" (or similar). Discovery: (a) engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave NOTE summarizing the April 2026 wave with 3 new relocations, surcharges, INV-2026-0410 for $15,900, and noting 5 total active Vectral relocations including Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei; (b) engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation NOTE documenting the final offer delivered to Rachel Whitfield (10% loyalty discount, 12-month extension, 8-relocation min, $2K/month stipend platform fee) with board decision expected around April 23.
OE 17: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Vectral using airtable_search_records (searching "Client Accounts" table in base appMoveOpsOps001 with field_name "Client" and value "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Discovery: Vectral Systems client record (recVectralSystems01) shows Account Manager Mina Hashimoto, Relationship Start 2024-11-20, Contract Type "Preferred Partner," Monthly Stipend Fee $2,000, Total Relocations 11, Status Active. Detailed notes document the full renewal negotiation timeline and current status (Renewal Pending), plus estimate EST-2026-0419 for $58,400 and revenue to date of $109,900.
